title: The World's Biggest Art Heist: Still a Mystery After 36 Years
summary:
Have you ever lost something valuable? Imagine losing 13 paintings worth more than $500 million! That's what happened at the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum in Boston back in 1990. It's the biggest art theft in history, and the paintings are still missing.

Here's how it went down: Two men dressed as police officers tricked the guards and tied them up in the basement. Then they went to the museum's Dutch Room and cut famous paintings like Vermeer's "The Concert" and Rembrandt's "Christ in the Storm on the Sea of Galilee" right out of their frames. They also took works by Degas and Manet, plus a little metal eagle decoration that wasn't worth much. They even stole the security tapes!

For 36 years, detectives have been trying to solve the case. A former FBI agent named Geoff Kelly wrote a book with new details. He thinks the thieves were connected to the Boston mob and maybe even the Irish Republican Army. Some suspects were killed or died in suspicious ways. One man named Bobby Donati was found stabbed to death in the trunk of a car. Another suspect, George Reissfelder, died under strange circumstances, and a painting that had been in his bedroom disappeared.

The empty frames still hang in the museum today, waiting for the paintings to come back. Will they ever be found? That's the million-dollar question—actually, the $500-million question!
why_it_matters: This story reminds us that some mysteries take a long time to solve, and we need to be patient and keep asking questions.

background_read: The Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum (a famous art museum in Boston, founded in 1903) has been waiting for its stolen artworks for more than 36 years.
Other famous unsolved art heists, like the theft of the Mona Lisa in 1911, were eventually solved after decades.
The Irish Republican Army (a group that fought for Irish independence) was suspected of being involved in some art thefts to raise money.
Article_Structure: WHO: Two thieves disguised as police officers.
WHAT: Stole 13 famous paintings worth over $500 million from the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum.
WHY: The crime remains unsolved after 36 years; detectives suspect ties to the Boston mob and Irish Republican Army.

perspectives.description: The Gardner Museum continues to display empty frames as a reminder of the loss, offering a reward for information and hoping for the paintings' return.
The case is frustrating because many suspects are dead or killed, and evidence is sparse; Geoff Kelly's new book brings fresh attention to the crime.
The theft is a major cultural loss, and similar unsolved heists have occasionally been solved after decades, giving a glimmer of hope that the paintings might still be found.
